issues.claim: the senior who has crossed the thicket can hear new architecture proposals... without being captured by any
issues.objection: Lexically clean. moha-kalilam = 'thicket of delusion' is correctly rendered (kalilam = dense mass, thicket, turbid water). The scoping of nirveda is preserved (indifference to the chain of phala-promising teachings, not generic disengagement). Shankara's reading of moha-kalilam as aviveka (non-discrimination between Self and not-Self) is recorded in the source pack; the engineering body simplifies toward the operational analog (thicket of architecture-meme proposals) without claiming that the verse's primary referent is architecture proposals. One framing nit: the verse's primary referent is the PRACTITIONER's own delusion-thicket (cognitive obstruction), not the proposals as external thicket. The engineering metaphor is subtly outward-projecting where the verse is inward. Acceptable as analog (the cognitive thicket and the proposal-chain are isomorphic in their effect on the buddhi), but a careful Sanskritist notes the directional shift.

notes: The engineering layer correctly preserves: (a) the SCOPED nature of nirveda — the source pack records all four traditions agreeing that the indifference is to a specific class (phala-promising teachings), not to all hearing/learning; (b) the verse's claim that nirveda is a NATURAL maturation, not cynicism — the engineering body explicitly says 'Saturation that lets new claims be heard without being captured by them. She has crossed the moha-kalilam'; (c) Sanskrit terminology (moha-kalilam, nirveda, śrotavyasya śrutasya) preserved in the engineering body. The falsifiability clause forecloses the most common misreading ('stop reading conference talks'). One minor framing note on the inward/outward thicket; not critical. Pass.
